task_completion.description: Agent navigates the map view to the Fairbanks, Alaska region.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Map view centered on Fairbanks (approx 64.84°N, 147.72°W). Look for city labels 'Fairbanks' or coordinates in the status bar.

task_completion.description: Agent activates the path measurement tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Measure', 'Ruler', or 'Path' tool is active in the interface, indicated by a dialog box, toolbar icon, or specific cursor mode.

task_completion.description: The measurement path originates at Fairbanks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The first point of the drawn path line is anchored at or very near the Fairbanks label on the map.

task_completion.description: The path follows the pipeline corridor southwards with multiple waypoints.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A line is drawn on the map with multiple intermediate waypoints (not a single straight line) heading South/South-East towards the coast, generally following the highway/pipeline route.

task_completion.description: The measurement path terminates at the Valdez Marine Terminal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The final point of the drawn path line is anchored at Valdez (approx 61.13°N, 146.35°W) on the southern coast of Alaska.

task_completion.description: Agent saves the path as a KML file with the correct name and location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog, file manager window, or application sidebar showing the filename 'TAPS_Fairbanks_Valdez.kml' saved in '/home/ga/Documents/'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a GUI mapping application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a mapping application (e.g., Marble, Google Earth) window is the primary focus, not a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the path was created via manual interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: The path shows irregularities or waypoints consistent with manual clicking (e.g., following the curve of the route), rather than a mathematically perfect straight line or generated curve.

integrity.description: Verify the output file was not created via terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: No evidence of commands like 'echo ... > file.kml' or 'cat > file.kml' being used to generate the file content directly in a terminal.
